prompt: Create a photorealistic historical scene in a wide, cinematic 19th-century style: an elderly bearded man in a black frock coat and tall top hat stands dramatically at the center on a rough wooden railroad track, legs spread in a triumphant pose, raising an oversized sledgehammer high above his head with both hands as if about to drive the final railroad spike. Around him are exactly 22 visible onlookers, mostly men in Victorian-era suits, waistcoats, bowler hats, top hats, cowboy hats, and one blue military officer uniform, arranged in a semicircle on both sides of the track; several hold canes or walking sticks, and some faces are partly obscured at the edges. In the foreground, steel rails curve inward toward a bright golden ceremonial spike or coupling fixture positioned between the rails. The setting is an open, arid plain with sparse scrub, backed by a majestic range of snow-covered mountains under a clear pale-blue sky with wispy clouds. Use realistic lighting, crisp documentary detail, slightly humorous surreal scale for the raised hammer, natural earth tones, black and brown period clothing, and a sharp focus on the central pose, resembling a staged commemorative photograph of the transcontinental railroad ceremony in the late 1800s.
